reasoning: The USDA FSIS timeline for closing a recall is historically lengthy. The recall was issued on April 5, 2025, and involves 22,672 lbs of product shipped to 10 states. FSIS must conduct effectiveness checks, verify that the recalling firm has retrieved and properly disposed of the products, and wait for comprehensive documentation before changing the status to Closed. This bureaucratic process typically takes several months, and often up to a year, especially for widespread recalls requiring significant verification. Consequently, the chance that the recall is formally marked "Closed" within just 87 days (by July 1, 2025) is low. I estimate the probability at 18%, reflecting the slow nature of government administrative procedures.
